name: Energy Assistance Program (EAP) / Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
source: MN Department of Commerce EAP Guidelines; 42 U.S.C. 8621 (LIHEAP); MN Statutes 216B
description:
The Energy Assistance Program (EAP) helps low-income Minnesotans pay their home heating costs and energy bills. The program is funded through the federal Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) and administered in Minnesota by the Department of Commerce through a network of local Community Action Program (CAP) agencies. EAP is designed to prevent dangerous heating shutoffs during Minnesota's cold winters and reduce the burden of high energy costs on low-income households.

EAP provides two main types of help. The regular heating benefit ranges from approximately $200 to $1,400 per heating season, depending on household income, heating fuel type, home size, and geographic area. Benefits are paid directly to the energy provider (utility company, propane supplier, fuel oil dealer, etc.). Households that rent and pay heat as part of their rent can also receive a benefit paid to the landlord.

In addition to the regular benefit, EAP includes a Crisis Fuel component for households that have run out of heating fuel or face an immediate shutoff. Crisis Fuel provides up to approximately $600 in emergency heating assistance and can be accessed even by households that have already received the regular benefit. Crisis Fuel is typically available from November through mid-May, subject to available funding.

The income eligibility threshold is 50% of the State Median Income (SMI). For 2024-2025, this is approximately: 1 person $2,538/month ($30,456/year); 2 people $3,316/month ($39,792/year); 3 people $4,094/month ($49,128/year); 4 people $4,872/month ($58,464/year). Thresholds are updated annually. There is no asset test.

Households that receive SNAP, MFIP, SSI, or Medical Assistance may be categorically eligible and can be processed quickly. All homeowners and renters are eligible — you do not need to pay your own utilities to qualify.

The program season typically runs from October 1 through May 31, though intake often opens in mid-October. Weatherization assistance — insulation, furnace tune-ups, and other energy efficiency improvements — is also available to EAP-eligible households through the Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP), which helps reduce future energy costs.

Applications are made at the local CAP agency serving your county. Wait lists can occur; early application is encouraged. Some counties offer home visits for elderly and disabled applicants.
eligibility_summary: Household income at or below 50% State Median Income (2024-2025 approximate: $2,538/month for 1 person, $3,316 for 2, $4,094 for 3, $4,872 for 4). Minnesota resident. Homeowner or renter. No asset test. SNAP/MFIP/SSI/MA recipients often auto-eligible. Season: October 1 - May 31.
benefit_amounts: Regular heating benefit: $200-$1,400 per season (varies by income, fuel type, home size). Crisis Fuel: up to $600 for emergency heating need. Paid directly to energy provider.
documents_required: Proof of identity, Social Security numbers for all household members, proof of Minnesota residency, proof of income for all household members (pay stubs, benefit letters), most recent energy bill or landlord contact if heat is included in rent.
contact: Contact your local Community Action Program (CAP) agency. Find your local agency at: mn.gov/commerce/energy-assistance or call 1-800-657-3710
